title: Prime, Composite, or Neither
goal: Repair the idea that 1 is prime and rebuild divisor-based classification.
firstTutorQuestion: How many divisors does 1 actually have?
successSignal: Learner says 1 is neither prime nor composite and gives a divisor-based reason.

title: Entry Bridge With 24
goal: Rebuild the habit of starting with an easy divisor pair instead of freezing.
firstTutorQuestion: What is the easiest divisor you can test first?
successSignal: Learner chooses 2 as a starting point for 24 and explains why.

title: Rewrite the Final Factorization Cleanly
goal: Stabilize the move from split-work to a complete final prime factorization.
firstTutorQuestion: Which factors should still remain in the final line?
successSignal: Learner writes one complete prime factorization with no missing or composite factor.

title: Verification Bridge With 12
goal: Teach the learner to check whether every factor is prime before accepting a factorization.
firstTutorQuestion: Before checking the product, are all the factors prime?
successSignal: Learner rejects 2 x 6 because 6 is not prime and chooses 2 x 2 x 3.

title: Reuse Factorization for Divisor Count
goal: Stop the learner from restarting divisor counting from scratch.
firstTutorQuestion: In this problem, do you need the factorization itself or the number of divisors?
successSignal: Learner starts from the existing prime factorization instead of listing divisors one by one.

title: Find the Missing Factor for a Square
goal: Connect prime-factor counts to square-number structure.
firstTutorQuestion: Which prime factor count is odd right now and needs one more?
successSignal: Learner identifies the missing factor from the prime factorization and explains why.

title: Sort Common Divisor vs Common Multiple
goal: Help the learner name which structure to look for before calculating.
firstTutorQuestion: Are you looking for what overlaps or what covers both numbers?
successSignal: Learner can say whether the task is about shared structure or covering structure before solving.

title: Explain Which Part of the Factorization Matters
goal: Make the learner point to the relevant part of the factorization before solving a transfer task.
firstTutorQuestion: Which part of the factorization helps you answer this question first?
successSignal: Learner identifies the relevant part of the factorization before using it.
